Class DBQueries

java.lang.Object
com.example.passmanager.DBQueries
public class DBQueries extends Object
This class is used to have a place where all SQL statements passed to the backend can live, although these are susceptible to SQL injections the next scope will be to change that

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    DBQueries()
    Constructs a new DBQueries instance.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    String
    addAccount(String platform, String username, String password, String salt)
    Returns an INSERT statement to add a new account record.
    String
    checkForAccount(String username, String platform)
    Returns a SELECT statement to check whether an account already exists for the given username and platform.
    String
    getAccountByUsername(String username, String platform)
    Returns a SELECT statement to retrieve the PASSWORD and RANDOM_SALT for an account matching the given username and platform.
    String
    getUsernameByID(int ID)
    Returns a SELECT statement to retrieve the USERNAME for a given account ID.
    String
    removeAccount(String platform, String username)
    Returns a DELETE statement to remove an account matching the platform and username.
    String
    resultSetToString(ResultSet rs)
    Reads the first row of a result set and returns its contents as a formatted string.
    String
    viewAccountInfo(String searchedFor)
    Returns a SELECT statement to find accounts matching a platform, username, or password.

    Methods inherited from class Object

    clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

  • Constructor Details

    • DBQueries

      public DBQueries()
      Constructs a new DBQueries instance.

  • Method Details

    • addAccount

      public String addAccount(String platform, String username, String password, String salt) throws Exception
      Returns an INSERT statement to add a new account record.
      Parameters:
      platform - the platform/service name
      username - the account username
      password - the encrypted password
      salt - the Base64-encoded salt used during encryption
      Returns:
      a SQL INSERT string for the Accounts table
      Throws:
      Exception - if the query cannot be constructed

    • removeAccount

      public String removeAccount(String platform, String username) throws Exception
      Returns a DELETE statement to remove an account matching the platform and username.
      Parameters:
      platform - the platform name to match
      username - the username to match
      Returns:
      a SQL DELETE string for the Accounts table
      Throws:
      Exception - if the query cannot be constructed

    • viewAccountInfo

      public String viewAccountInfo(String searchedFor) throws Exception
      Returns a SELECT statement to find accounts matching a platform, username, or password.
      Parameters:
      searchedFor - the value to search across Platform, Username, and Password columns
      Returns:
      a SQL SELECT string for the Accounts table
      Throws:
      Exception - if the query cannot be constructed

    • checkForAccount

      public String checkForAccount(String username, String platform) throws Exception
      Returns a SELECT statement to check whether an account already exists for the given username and platform.
      Parameters:
      username - the username to look up
      platform - the platform name to match
      Returns:
      a SQL SELECT string for the Accounts table
      Throws:
      Exception - if the query cannot be constructed

    • getAccountByUsername

      public String getAccountByUsername(String username, String platform) throws Exception
      Returns a SELECT statement to retrieve the PASSWORD and RANDOM_SALT for an account matching the given username and platform.
      Parameters:
      username - the username to look up
      platform - the platform name to match
      Returns:
      a SQL SELECT string returning PASSWORD and RANDOM_SALT columns
      Throws:
      Exception - if the query cannot be constructed

    • getUsernameByID

      public String getUsernameByID(int ID) throws Exception
      Returns a SELECT statement to retrieve the USERNAME for a given account ID.
      Parameters:
      ID - the account ID to look up
      Returns:
      a SQL SELECT string returning the USERNAME column
      Throws:
      Exception - if the query cannot be constructed

    • resultSetToString

      public String resultSetToString(ResultSet rs)
      Reads the first row of a result set and returns its contents as a formatted string.
      Parameters:
      rs - the result set to read from
      Returns:
      a string containing ID, Platform, Username, Password, and Salt from the first row, or an empty string if the result set is empty
